title: anthemis arvensis subsp. incrassata
intro: Anthemis arvensis subsp. incrassata, commonly known as chamomile, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. The health benefits of chamomile include improved sleep quality, reduced menstrual cramps, and relief from symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal preparations of chamomile, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
